title: Device Requirements, Compatibility, Serviceability, and Asset Baselines · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic device requirements, compatibility, serviceability, and asset baselines ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in requirements-to-compatible-change matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements.
Interpret decimal and binary capacity, frequency, transfer rate, latency, dimensions, connector markings, model identifiers, and tolerance without false precision.
Trace physical fit, electrical limits, protocol support, firmware, drivers, operating system, application, policy, and warranty as one compatibility chain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types
steps.instruction:
debug case — A low-cost upgrade physically fits but conflicts with firmware, power, application, accessibility, warranty, lifecycle, and recovery requirements. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements.

Inspect the Requirement Types trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types, keep Requirement Types connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Requirement Types, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Requirement Types precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A low-cost upgrade physically fits but conflicts with firmware, power, application, accessibility, warranty, lifecycle, and recovery requirements. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements.

For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirement Types, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A vendor minimum-requirements list describes a usable and supportable system.

stimulus.title: Requirement Types case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A low-cost upgrade physically fits but conflicts with firmware, power, application, accessibility, warranty, lifecycle, and recovery requirements. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A vendor minimum-requirements list describes a usable and supportable system.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate functional, performance, capacity, interface, security, accessibility, support, environmental, cost, and lifecycle requirements. Artifact under review: requirements-to-compatible-change matrix.

options.text: The Requirement Types record says requirements-to-compatible-change matrix looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Requirement Types.

For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Requirement Types for requirements-to-compatible-change matrix.
The record names the assumption, applies Requirement Types, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Asset Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append a fictional support incident block headed "support-asset-baseline-cpseek:" with concrete user impact, safety and authority, competing hypothesis, bounded test, observation, repair and rollback, changed-case verification, and escalation evidence.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Asset Baseline.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Asset Baseline into observable performance evidence. For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Asset Baseline, keep Asset Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
support-asset-baseline-cpseek:
  user-impact: [replace with the bounded user outcome]
  safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, and stop rule]
  hypothesis: [replace with a falsifiable cause and competing explanation]
  test: [replace with one reversible discriminating simulator test]
  observation: [replace with the actual result and rejected inference]
  repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair and rollback trigger]
  verify: [replace with original, changed, and side-effect checks]
  escalation: [replace with threshold, destination, and evidence packet]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Asset Baseline structural check is only one gate. requirements-to-compatible-change matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create an authorized privacy-minimized asset baseline with identity, configuration, condition, ownership, support state, and evidence provenance.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Asset Baseline, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Asset Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Asset Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A low-cost upgrade physically fits but conflicts with firmware, power, application, accessibility, warranty, lifecycle, and recovery requirements. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Create an authorized privacy-minimized asset baseline with identity, configuration, condition, ownership, support state, and evidence provenance.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Asset Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A hostname alone uniquely and durably identifies an asset.
